_description: Example config for cs_importer. Copy to <your_data>.json alongside your XLSX file.
_warning: NEVER commit a config file containing real credentials or company paths.
_comment_ws_columns: 0-based column indices in the Workspace sheet.
_comment_file_path_resolution: OPTION A – Legacy single root (original behaviour):
Set local_file_root to an absolute path. auto_detect_file_columns can be false.
Set local_file_roots to a list of paths. The importer tries each in order.
UNC paths work fine on Windows: \\\\server\\share\\folder
OPTION C – Full auto-detect (no fixed columns):
Set auto_detect_file_columns: true and leave file_columns empty ({}).
The importer scans up to 30 rows to find the header row automatically.
Add file_column_aliases for headers not in the built-in English/Hungarian list.
All options are backward-compatible. Any file_columns values you DO specify
override auto-detection for that field only.
_comment_file_columns: Specify 0-based column indices to override auto-detection for individual fields.
Leave as {} to rely entirely on header auto-detection.
_comment_category_fields: Maps a semantic field key to its CS AttrID and XLSX column(s).
multi_value fields use col_start/col_end; single-value fields use col.
value_map translates short XLSX values to the full CS picklist values.
